name: generateSecretKey
description: Creates a secret encryption key that can be used with the encrypt() and decrypt() functions.

params.name: algorithm
params.description: Encryption algorithm used to generate the key. Common values are AES, DES, BLOWFISH, and DESEDE (Triple DES). The generated key cannot be used with the default CFMX_COMPAT algorithm.

params.name: keysize
params.description: Optional key size in bits for the generated encryption key. Common AES key sizes are 128, 192, and 256. If this value is not provided, the default size depends on the Java version used by the server. For example, Java 19 and later may automatically generate 256-bit AES keys instead of 128-bit keys.

coldfusion.notes: ColdFusion 2025 running on Java 19 or later may generate AES 256-bit keys by default when keysize is not specified.

lucee.notes: Available encryption algorithms may vary depending on the Java version and JVM configuration.

links.title: Generating AES 256 Bit Keys
links.description: More information about AES 256-bit encryption support in Java.

examples.title: Generate an AES Key
examples.description: Generate a secret key and use it to encrypt and decrypt a message.
examples.result: Displays the encrypted and decrypted message.
